_meta.vintageNote: 2026 ATPs used for Mathematics and NS&Tech where available; 2023-24 ATPs used for all other subjects (still current per DBE).

discriminators.topic-blocks: Maths / NST / Technology / EMS — week-banded topic blocks with explicit hours per topic.
discriminators.language-strands: Home Language and First Additional Language — fortnightly grids across Listening/Speaking, Reading/Viewing, Writing/Presenting, Language Structures.
discriminators.themed: Life Skills / Life Orientation — term-long theme with weekly sub-activities and a resources list.

_meta.pilotStatus: Mathematics Grade 4 Term 1 only — pending sign-off before batching the remaining 32 PDFs.

blocks.topic: Whole numbers: counting, ordering, comparing, representing and place value
concepts: Revision of Grade 3 work (integrated into the topics accordingly)
Count forwards and backwards in 2s, 3s, 5s, 10s, 25s, 50s, 100s between 0 and at least 10 000
Order, compare and represent numbers to at least four-digit numbers
Represent odd and even numbers to at least 1 000
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least four-digit numbers
Round off to the nearest 10, 100 and 1 000
prerequisite: Counting, ordering, comparing and representing the place value of three-digit numbers up to 800
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least three-digit numbers up to 800

blocks.topic: Number sentences and properties of whole numbers
concepts: Write number sentences to describe problem situations
Solve and complete number sentences by inspection, trial and improvement, and checking the solution by substitution
Recognise and use the commutative, associative and distributive properties of operations with whole numbers
0 in terms of its additive property

blocks.topic: Whole numbers: addition and subtraction (4-digit numbers)
concepts: Number range for calculations: addition and subtraction of whole numbers of at least 4 digits
Use any two calculation techniques to perform and check written and mental calculations
Calculation techniques: estimation; building up and breaking down numbers; using a number line; rounding off and compensating; using addition and subtraction as inverse operations
Solving problems involving whole numbers in financial contexts
Solving problems involving whole numbers in measurement contexts
Note: ensure that the strategies used do not compromise conceptual understanding
prerequisite: Counting, ordering, comparing and representing the place value of three-digit numbers up to 800
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least 800
Round off to the nearest 10, 100 and 1 000
Adding and subtracting units, multiples of 10 and multiples of 100 to/from any three-digit number up to 800

scope: Whole numbers: counting, ordering and representing numbers
blocks.notes: Assignment to be completed in class within three hours.

blocks.topic: Whole numbers: multiplication, multiples and factors
concepts: Number range for calculations: multiplication of one-digit numbers by numbers up to 10
Multiplication of at least a two-digit number by a one-digit number
Multiplication of at least a whole two-digit number by two-digit numbers
Calculation techniques: estimation; building up and breaking down numbers; doubling and halving; using multiplication and division as inverse operations
Multiples of one-digit numbers to at least 100
Factors of one-digit whole numbers to at least 10
Factors of two-digit whole numbers to at least 100
Solving problems involving whole numbers in financial contexts
Solving problems involving whole numbers in measurement contexts
Solving problems comparing two or more quantities of the same kind (ratio)
Solving problems comparing two quantities of different kinds (rate)
Note: ensure that the strategies used do not compromise conceptual understanding
prerequisite: Multiplication facts for units by multiples of 10 and 100
Building up and breaking down three-digit whole numbers
Round off to the nearest 10 and estimate answers
